anpia_only_duplicate_check.method: Live SELECT on tech_team_outputs.ph_task, scoped exclusively to project_code = 'ANPIA' (exact and case-insensitive) and project_code='ANPIA' AND assigned_user='Nivarnan'. project_code = 'AGE' was never queried in this task.
anpia_only_duplicate_check.conclusion: No ANPIA row of any kind exists. There is no same-day row, no prior version, and nothing to update or replace.

proposed_action_rationale: Zero ANPIA rows exist under any query scoped to project_code='ANPIA', with or without the assigned_user filter. This is an unambiguous new-insert case -- not a same-day update/replace scenario. (The earlier ambiguity encountered in a prior task run, caused by comparing against an unrelated AGE-project row, does not apply here: this task's instructions explicitly established that AGE belongs to different work and must be excluded from every decision.)

one_active_row_rule.explanation: ph_task has no boolean 'active' column and no unique constraint that would enforce a single active row per project_code+assigned_user+date at the database level. 'One active same-day ANPIA publication' is a process convention that must be enforced by the publishing script's own duplicate check (see 05_IMPLEMENTATION/publish_ph_task_production_report.py) and by human review, not by the schema.

approval_record.approved_by: User (Nivarnan/report owner, via explicit chat instruction)
approval_record.approval_scope: All proposed ph_task publication column values -- task_name, task_id, team, developer, phase_level, assigned_user_team, version_level, version_status.
approval_record.note: All previously LOW/MEDIUM/NONE-confidence fields were updated to the user-approved values above and marked HIGH confidence / reviewer_approval_needed=false. This manifest edit was made immediately prior to computing the --approved-manifest-sha256 used to authorize execution, per the user's own explicit execution instruction ('Calculate the live SHA-256 of the manifest').

known_limitations: No dedicated report-date, filename, or path column exists in ph_task -- the production filename and report date are not stored in any structured column. If needed for downstream tracking, they must be included in task_name/task_id/description text.

task_id uniqueness is not database-enforced (no UNIQUE constraint live) -- even if a task_id is assigned later, it cannot be relied on alone for de-duplication. task_id remains NULL for this row per the approved instruction.
'One active same-day ANPIA publication' is a process rule, not a database constraint -- enforced by the publishing script's live duplicate check and by this approval, not by the schema.
